p.seg: To all sons and faithful people of Holy Mother Church, Edgar son of Donald sends Greeting:

Let all people, both present and future, know that, with the consent of my son and heir, Fergus, I have given and granted, and by this present charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, the church of Dalgarnock with all the land pertaining to the same church, and with common pasture of the foresaid toun, and all its other revenues and pertinents, in teinds as well as in other obventions, justly belonging to the same church, for them to hold in free, pure and perpetual alms, for the salvation of the souls of my lords the kings of Scots, and for the souls of my father and my mother and of all my ancestors and successors. Wherefore I will that the foresaid canons hold and possess the forementioned church with its land and all its liberties so freely, quietly, fully and honourably in perpetuity as any church in the whole of the kingdom of the Scots is freely given, is quietly and fully held and possessed, reserving the parsonage of G. the parson, and the perpetual vicarage of Enoch.

With these witnesses, Fergus son of the lord Edgar, Martin the parson of Dumfries, Enoch the clerk, John son of Ralph the dean of Dumfries, Gilchrist the judex, Gilla Conaill and Donald sons of Edgar, and many other men of good testimony.
